title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 476.010 - Courts of record
text:
476.010.  Courts of record. — The supreme court of the state of Missouri, the court of appeals, and the circuit courts shall be courts of record, and shall keep just and faithful records of their proceedings.  Notwithstanding the foregoing, municipal divisions of the circuit courts shall not be considered courts of record, regardless of whether or not a verbatim record of proceedings before the division is kept.
(RSMo 1939 § 1990, A.L. 1945 p. 806, A.L. 1973 S.B. 263, A.L. 1978 H.B. 1634, A.L. 1997 S.B. 248, A.L. 2001 S.B. 267) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 1826; 1919 § 2323; 1909 § 3845 
Effect of 1945 constitution on existing courts, Const. schedule § 4 (Schedule follows Const. Art. XII) 
Judicial power, where vested, Const. Art. V § 1
